meaning: adj.不均衡的,错乱的,不稳定的,收支不平衡的
examples: The centre of pressure moves rearward and the aeroplane becomes unbalanced.
The distribution of resources is unbalanced between rural and urban schools.
I knew how unbalanced Paula had been since my uncle Peter died.
If it was unbalanced, it was unbalanced in the direction of not enough revenue.
But with little knowledge of nutritional values, their diets are now unbalanced and unhealthy.
